display.purpose: Car name / page-level heading, once per screen.

headline.purpose: Section headers within the option side panel.

title.purpose: Uppercase category labels and current-selection indicators.

shadows.name: rim-separation
shadows.purpose: Lit top edge on cards/panels instead of a floating-card shadow.

shadows.name: selection-glow
shadows.purpose: The only true shadow in the system — reserved for selected/active state.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus indicator, crisp, no blur.

motion.name: ease-default
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA — Share Build, confirm actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2f5ce8; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.1s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1e46c9; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #2f5ce8; }

components.name: Option Card — Selected
components.description: The core building block of the side panel option list, selected state.
components.css: .ds-option-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #1f2937; border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px; border: 1px solid transparent; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.06); text-align: left; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-option-card--selected { background: #111827; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.06), 0 0 0 2px #2f5ce8, 0 0 24px -8px #2f5ce8; } .ds-option-card__name { color: #ffffff; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-option-card__price { color: #9ca3af; font-size: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Option Card — Unavailable
components.description: Disabled/incompatible option — stays visible with inline explanation rather than being hidden.

components.name: Side Panel Category Switcher
components.description: Persistent category tabs (Trim / Color / Wheels / Interior / Packages) replacing the long-scroll stacked layout.
components.css: .ds-category-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-category-tab { background: transparent; border: none; color: #6b7280; text-align: left; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.2s, background 0.2s; } .ds-category-tab--active { color: #ffffff; background: #1f2937; }

narrative.overview: What it feels like to stand in front of the car under gallery lighting, configuration options laid out like a spec sheet on a podium beside it. The system is dark, confident, and exact — numbers are real, options are named and explained, depth comes from light, never drop shadow.
keyCharacteristics: Dark void background (#030712) so paint colors and chrome read as the brightest elements on screen
One accent — Electric Cobalt — used sparingly for selection, focus, and price emphasis only
Flat surfaces separated by light (borders, rim glow, inset highlights), never by drop shadow
Every number is real and precise; no vague iconography standing in for information

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Electric Cobalt appears only on selection state, primary actions, and price. It never decorates a heading or tints a background wash.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: No second typeface is introduced for personality. Hierarchy is entirely weight + size + tracking.

rules.name: The No-Card-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Nothing gets a soft ambient drop shadow for depth. Separation comes from borders, background-color steps, or the selection glow.

dos: Do keep the vehicle preview as the largest, most persistent element on screen
Do use Electric Cobalt exclusively for selection state, primary actions, and price
Do separate surfaces with borders, background-color steps, or the selection glow
Do show unavailable options in place with an inline explanation
Do keep one typeface across every role
donts: Don't build this as generic e-commerce product-options UI
Don't stack all five option categories as one long scrolling column
Don't reach for SaaS dashboard conventions
Don't use color alone to indicate selected/disabled state
Don't add soft ambient card shadows anywhere in the dark configurator shell
